ops.title: Error codes propagate across codeexec wire boundary to exit taxonomy
ops.body:
Error categories must survive the Python→Go boundary via the `err_code` wire field and `[ccx:CODE]` tags. Multiple not-found sentinels map to the shared `not_found` code, which joins the CLI exit taxonomy (`codeexec.ErrNotFound` → exit 3; see `internal/cli/errors.go` and commit d4e8426df6de "fix(codeexec): exec exits 3 on not-found").

Known leak in this design: stringified errors expose the `[ccx:CODE]` tags. Understand both the wire field and the tag lane before touching this path.

(Captured from the landing session's hook summary; that session owns the design detail — see `ccx vcs show d4e8426df6de` for the implementation.)
ops.when: when modifying codeexec error handling, exit codes, or the driver wire protocol
